topic: Extract the flywheel engine to a properly publishable form — github.com/JYeswak/flywheel for developers; flywheel.zeststream.ai for SMB clients; both build trust in Joshua, ZestStream brand, and the work.
audit_blocker_reason: 3 TRUE-blocker-class findings: live-state artifact denylist absent (Class 5), halted propagator scripts disposal unspecified (Class 6), H3 case-study consent workflow unspecified (Class 4). 4 proposed remediations need Joshua-ratification before Phase 4 fires.

hypothesis_slate.strategy: Aggressive extraction (maximal engine): extract 70-80% of current substrate as engine, spend 40-60h on de-personalization, ship comprehensive v0.2 covering doctrine + L-rules + memory + scripts + hooks + templates + skills.
hypothesis_slate.kill_condition: Any single substrate category exceeds 30 worker-hours of extraction effort, OR any de-personalization sweep produces an artifact that fails Joshua's 'would I direct a developer to this?' judged review.

hypothesis_slate.strategy: Minimal extraction (thin engine): extract only the ~11 pure-pattern memory rules + ~25 engine-class doctrines + 4 canonical hooks + install scaffold, ship minimal but solid v0.2, iterate to v0.3/v0.4 as adopters request expansion.
hypothesis_slate.kill_condition: Adopters' first-week feedback says 'this doesn't have enough substance to be useful' OR Joshua reviews and says 'this isn't proud-worthy yet.'

hypothesis_slate.strategy: Engine + redacted-overlay-as-case-study (both-and): publish the engine at developer-ready depth (~40 engine doctrines + 70 L-rules + 4 canonical hooks + ~80 scripts + ~30 memory rules + install scaffold) AND publish a redacted-but-real version of Joshua's overlay as the flagship case study on flywheel.zeststream.ai/case-studies. Engine builds developer trust; case study builds SMB trust.
hypothesis_slate.kill_condition: The redaction sweep on the overlay-as-case-study reveals so much real client data that even with redaction it can't ship without per-client consent we don't have time to collect, AND the engine-without-case-study fails the SMB-trust bar (no named outcomes on the webpage).

_notes: Dispatch transport: Agent() sub-agents (general-purpose) instead of codex worker panes. Pane state UNKNOWN in this session; pane-dispatch unsafe without verified WAITING state.
Convergence test unchanged: all 3 lanes A/B/C required for Phase 2 entry.
v0.1-rough-drafts/ in this dir is informative reference, not canonical; demoted from premature public-share/ by 346f2ec.

_phase1_summary.lane_a_finding: 94 doctrines, 109 L-rules, 183 memory rules; ~50% of scripts hardcode /Users/josh; 64% of doctrines name a client; 69% of memory rules name Joshua; sweep effort 40-60 hours
_phase1_summary.lane_b_finding: PAI is the closest reference; flywheel ahead on coordination + atomicity; must close on one-liner install + onboarding ritual + named case studies for SMB trust
_phase1_summary.lane_c_finding: 4-phase extraction pipeline; bash installer with staging-dir-rename atomicity; Next.js webpage on Vercel; 15-bead DAG with critical path B1→B2→...→B15; 12 open design questions
